description: Internet-based therapy for the Danish psychiatric system
challenge: Millions of citizens fall into a treatment gap — too unwell for general practice, but not sick enough to be referred to a psychiatric department. Denmark needed a shared national platform to deliver digital therapy at scale.
role: Product Designer — product strategy, user research, concept development, wireframing, prototyping, and user testing across three interconnected platforms.
outcome: Currently in development as the designated national solution for online mental health treatment in Denmark. Designed to meet WCAG and MDR requirements.

description: Cooking inspiration platform for Arla in Sweden
challenge: Arla Sweden has a huge library of recipes designed to inspire people to cook more. But the experience wasn't personal enough — and engagement and retention were declining.
role: Design Lead — product vision and strategy, concept development, A/B testing, and analytics.
outcome: 5x increase in cooking activity, retention stabilised after a declining trend, and the app went from a ranking of 72 to 33 in the App Store.

description: Tablet-based electronic health record for the ward
challenge: A national tender for a next-generation patient record system — designed for tablet, built on modern technology, and aimed at making clinicians' daily work easier.
role: Design Lead — concept development, clinical narrative, interactive prototype, and pitch delivery.
outcome: Selected as one of 2 out of 35 companies to continue the dialogue with the national health ministry.

challenge: New customers were left waiting without knowing their status, bounced between employees, and forced to chase the bank themselves. Many lost patience before they even got started.
role: Product Designer — user research, mystery shopping, journey mapping, design sprint facilitation, prototyping, and user testing.
outcome: The initial sprint led to a year-long engagement where the solution was developed and launched. It has since been sold to an organisation serving 8 major Danish banks.

description: AI tool helping clinicians identify patients at risk of acute hospitalization
challenge: Enversion had built an AI model that could predict the risk of acute hospitalisation. The technology worked — but when pitched to GPs, there was little interest. The model needed a real problem to solve.
role: Product Designer — market research, literature review, fieldwork, interviews, product vision and strategy.
outcome: Identified multiple validated use cases including triage of out-of-hours calls and resource prioritisation in emergency departments — directly impacting length of stay and admission rates.

challenge: Paper medication leaflets are one of the most important patient safety tools we have — and almost nobody reads them. The current format fails both patients and pharmaceutical companies.
role: Lead Designer — concept development, prototyping, user testing, and validation across two research rounds within the MinLæge app.
outcome: Validated with 135 patients through unmoderated testing and surveys, achieving strong SUS and NPS scores.

description: Published research on what makes telemedicine commercially sustainable
challenge: Denmark produces a lot of telemedicine research. But very few projects make the leap from research to a sustainable product. The question was: what separates the ones that survive?
role: Sole Researcher — project planning, case identification, qualitative interviews, business model analysis, and peer-reviewed publication.
outcome: Published in Health Services Management Research. Identified the business model patterns that distinguish commercially sustainable telemedicine.
